texto: There is no compulsion for man to accept the truth. But it is certainly a shame upon the human intellect when man is not even interested in finding out as to what is the truth! Islam teaches that God has given man the faculty of reason and therefore expects man to reason things out objectively and systematically for himself. To reflect and to question and to reflect.
explicacao: Citação de Maurice Bucaille sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “There is no compulsion for man to accept the truth. But it is certainly a shame upon the human in…” articula reflexao, freedom, islam em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como islam e verdade.

frase_original: There is no compulsion for man to accept the truth. But it is certainly a shame upon the human intellect when man is not even interested in finding out as to what is the truth! Islam teaches that God has given man the faculty of reason and therefore expects man to reason things out objectively and systematically for himself. To reflect and to question and to reflect.
